attackSurface.notes: Function recv_internal may be an attack surface
Function on_bounce may be an attack surface
Function recv_external may be an attack surface

exploits.title: Reentrancy via External Call Before State Update
exploits.prerequisites: Attacker can call the withdraw/transfer function
steps: Attacker calls the function that sends a message
External message is dispatched before balance is updated
Attacker re-enters the contract while old balance is still stored
Attacker drains funds by repeating the withdrawal
exploits.tonSpecificNotes: In TON the actor model means reentrancy happens via chained messages, not recursive calls — the state update must happen before any send_raw_message.

exploits.title: Unauthorized Access via Unprotected recv_external
exploits.prerequisites: Attacker can send an external message to the contract
steps: Attacker crafts an external message with any desired op-code
recv_external calls accept_message() with no sender verification
Attacker executes privileged operations (balance reset, ownership change)
exploits.expectedImpact: Arbitrary state manipulation — attacker can set balance to any value or take ownership
exploits.tonSpecificNotes: recv_external accepts messages from any off-chain source; without a signature or owner check any actor can trigger admin functions.

response.summary: Hacker Mode analysis identified 3 attack surface(s). 2 plausible exploits found, 0 theoretical exploits identified. Hacker Resilience Score: 30/100.

recommendations.mitigation: Add reentrancy guards and ensure state changes happen before external calls. Use message mode 64 for bounce protection.
Add proper access control checks using throw_unless and equal_slices to verify sender identity.
